senses.definition: to make less progress than other competitors

examples.x: Despite their best efforts, the team began to fall behind in the race as other competitors surged ahead.
The student struggled with the complex concepts and started to fall behind the rest of the class.
Economic challenges caused the small business to fall behind larger competitors in terms of technological advancements.
Regular maintenance is crucial; otherwise, your car's performance may start to fall behind over time.
The company invested in employee training to ensure no one would fall behind in the rapidly changing industry.

senses.definition: to not succeed in doing something within the expected or required time limit

examples.x: When a team member got behind on tasks, the rest of the group had to help catch up.
Due to the unexpected obstacles, the project began to get behind.
He got behind on his coursework and needed to catch up before the exams.
Getting behind on assignments can lead to lower grades in school.
She started the project late and struggled to catch up, fearing she would get behind.

senses.definition: to develop or progress more slowly than someone or something else

examples.x: American companies are lagging behind their Chinese counterparts in the development of artificial intelligence.
The student is lagging behind in his studies and needs extra help.
The team is lagging behind in the standings and needs to start winning games.
If we don't invest in education, our children will lag behind their peers in other countries.
The team lagged behind in the first half of the game, but they came back to win in the second half.

senses.definition: to leave without taking someone or something with one

examples.x: The fleeing family was forced to leave behind their belongings in their hurried escape.
The soldier had left behind his comrades in the battle.
The family left behind their belongings in the rush to evacuate the burning building.

senses.definition: to be the true cause of something, often not immediately apparent

examples.x: The search for power and control often lies behind manipulative behavior and toxic relationships.
The unspoken fears lying behind the student's avoidance of public speaking were gradually revealed through therapy.
The hidden agenda lying behind the politician's actions raised concerns among the public.
The underlying prejudice lying behind the discriminatory policies had to be confronted and dismantled for true equality to prevail.
The subconscious belief of unworthiness often lies behind self-sabotage and self-destructive patterns.

senses.definition: to remain in a location while others depart

examples.x: The brave firefighter stayed behind in the burning building to ensure everyone was safely evacuated.
Despite the storm, the dedicated teacher stayed behind in the classroom to help a struggling student.
The soldier stayed behind to provide cover fire while the rest of the squad retreated.
The determined scientist stayed behind in the laboratory to conduct additional experiments after the research team departed.
The dedicated volunteer stayed behind at the shelter to help with feeding and caring for the animals after visiting hours ended.

senses.definition: to discover, meet, or find someone or something by accident

examples.x: While cleaning out the attic, I came across an old box of photographs from my childhood.
During my walk in the park, I came across an injured bird and decided to take it to a wildlife rescue center.
I was browsing through a bookstore when I came across a novel that caught my attention.
While researching for my project, I came across an interesting article that provided valuable insights.
I didn't expect to come across an old friend from high school at the conference, but it was a pleasant surprise.

senses.definition: to be clearly understood or communicated

examples.x: The comedian's humor doesn't always get across to everyone in the audience.
The importance of teamwork needs to get across to all employees.
The speaker used simple language to ensure her message got across.
Non-verbal cues can help emotions get across even without words.
In a global company, cultural differences can affect how messages get across.

senses.definition: to remain well-informed about a particular topic, subject, or situation

examples.x: She likes to keep across scientific research to stay updated on breakthroughs.
In my job, it's essential to keep across the latest industry trends.
He makes an effort to keep across current events by reading the news daily.
As a student, it's crucial to keep across the course materials to excel in exams.
The manager expects the team to keep across the project's progress at all times.

senses.definition: to present information clearly and effectively to others

examples.x: During his speech, the mayor put across the importance of community involvement.
The teacher struggled to put across the new concept to his students.
It's crucial for advertisers to put across their message effectively.
I ran across an old friend from high school at the mall yesterday.
On my way to the store, I ran across an old neighbor I hadn't seen in years.
While attending a concert, I ran across a distant relative I hadn't seen in years.
During the neighborhood block party, I ran across my childhood friend who had recently moved back to town.
During the conference, I ran across a renowned expert in the field of astrophysics.
